relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Pay close attention so the heard word does not drift away.
So great a healing must not be neglected.
The Divine bore witness through signs, powers, and Spirit-gifts.
The coming inhabited world is not arranged under messengers.
Scripture asks: What is human, that Divine remembers?
Humanity is crowned with glory and honor.
All is arranged beneath human vocation, though not yet fully seen.
We see Jesus crowned through suffering, tasting death for everyone.
The pioneer of healing is completed through suffering into many children’s glory.
Sanctifier and sanctified are one family.
The Son sings Divine name among siblings.
Trust and children are held together before Divine.
Sharing flesh and blood, he breaks death’s enslaving power.
Fear of death releases its lifelong grip.
He takes hold of Abraham’s seed.
Made like the siblings, he becomes merciful and trustworthy high priest.
Having been tested, he helps the tested.

contemplative_note: Hebrews 2 is solidarity: the Son shares flesh and blood, sings among siblings, breaks fear of death, and becomes merciful help for tested humanity.
